entity.description: Definition of a parameter or variable and its metadata. It can represent a variable in a mathematical model or a device parameter.

field.name: parentParameterDefId
field.description: To model a hierarchical relationship between parameters.

field.name: externalId
field.description: ID of the parameter in an external system.

entity.description: Represents a parameter instance for the configuration of a math model, a solver, a physical device or a device group.

field.name: parameterUomId
field.description: Unit of measure for the parameter value.

field.name: parameterEnumId
field.description: The parameter actual enumerative value. For example device status word bit combinations, device control word bit combinations, etc. The default value is null.

entity.description: Represents a vector. You can also represent complex vectors in various ways, by separating the real and imaginary components into distinct vectors. Another approach is using a vector of vectors, where each element in the complex vector is represented as a 2D vector, where the first element is the real part and the second is the imaginary part. This last method can be extended to other contexts.

field.name: parentVectorId
field.description: The parent vector of which the current vector is a component or derive from.

field.name: coordinateSystemId
field.description: Coordinate system in which the vector is expressed.

field.name: dimension
field.description: Vector dimension.

field.name: magnitude
field.description: Magnitude of the vector. The magnitude (length) of the vector is calculated by the square root of the sum of the squares of its components.

entity.description: Each vector has components, one for each dimension of the vector space it belongs to. It can be canonical (basis-independent) or expressed in a specific coordinate system. Used only for row-wise access.

field.name: dimensionIndex
field.description: Dimension index for the vector.

field.name: parentComponentId
field.description: Link to the canonical component if this is derived from it.

field.name: projection
field.description: Directional projection is the orthogonal projection of the vector onto a particular axis or basis vector. Only valid for numerical vector.

field.name: rejection
field.description: Directional rejection is the perpendicular projection of the vector with respect to a particular axis or basis vector. Only valid for numerical vector.

entity.description: Represents a matrix. A matrix can be viewed as a linear transformation between two vector spaces or as a collection of vectors. For example, it could be either a rotation matrix, an N-dimensional translation vector or a complete rototranslation matrix. Using block matrices can be an effective way to represent tensors, matrices having complex numbers or, more generally, vectors, as elements.

field.name: parentMatrixId
field.description: The matrix from which the current matrix is derived. For example, for approximated matrices the original matrix from which it is computed.

field.name: coordinateSystemId
field.description: Coordinate system in which the matrix is expressed.

field.name: size
field.description: The matrix size represented as a string for display purpose. A matrix with m rows and n columns is called an m × n matrix.

field.name: rank
field.description: The rank of a matrix A is the dimension of the vector space generated (or spanned) by its columns.

field.name: determinant
field.description: The determinant of a square matrix. Its value characterizes some properties of the matrix and the linear map represented, on a given basis, by the matrix. In particular, the determinant is nonzero if and only if the matrix is invertible and the corresponding linear map is an isomorphism. In geometry it is the signed n-dimensional volume of a n-dimensional parallelepiped.

field.name: trace
field.description: The trace of a square matrix is defined to be the sum of elements on the main diagonal (from the upper left to the lower right) of the matrix. The trace is only defined for a square matrix (n × n).

field.name: conditionNumber
field.description: The condition number of the numerical stability of the linear system AX = B with respect to a matrix norm. See conditionNormEnumId

field.name: conditionNormEnumId
field.description: Which norm was used to compute conditionNumber (e.g., 1, 2, inf).

entity.description: Stores matrix components, either in canonical form or expressed in coordinate systems. Used for rotation matrices, Jacobians, transformation matrices, etc.

field.name: rowIndex
field.description: Row index for the matrix.

field.name: colIndex
field.description: Column index for the matrix.

entity.description: Generic n-dimensional tensor.

field.name: parentTensorId
field.description: The parent of which the current tensor is a component or from which it is derived.

field.name: coordinateSystemId
field.description: Coordinate system in which the tensor is expressed.

field.name: size
field.description: Total elements.

field.name: rank
field.description: Number of modes (axes) of the tensor.

field.name: shape
field.description: Convenience field materialised and derived from TensorAxis. JSON array of mode sizes, e.g. [32,3,224,224]. Matches Google TensorStore schema.

field.name: strides
field.description: Convenience field materialised and derived from TensorAxis. A stride is the jump necessary to go from one element to the next one in the specified dimension. The strides field is a pre-computed helper in the format [stride0, stride1, stride2, etc.].

field.name: nnz
field.description: Cached count of stored non-zero sparse entries (sparse tensors only).

field.name: frobeniusNorm
field.description: The Frobenius norm of any finite-order tensor measures its size by treating all entries as one long vector and taking the usual Euclidean 2-norm. Update only for frozen tensors.

field.name: memoryFormatEnumId
field.description: Logical in-memory layout / index order of dense tensor storage.

field.name: isPinned
field.description: Pinned-memory flag. Mostly CUDA host-to-device.

moqui.basic.Enumeration.description: Elements stored as an array field
Elements stored as a BLOB field
Elements stored as an external content

moqui.basic.EnumerationType.description: Tensor memory Format / Layout - In-Memory Logical Index Order

entity.description: Links a tensor to one or more content records (Zarr chunk, NPY, safetensors, etc.). Either the tensor has components or a content blob, not both.

field.name: axisSize
field.description: Length of the axis (0-based max index + 1).

field.name: axisStride
field.description: Row-major stride = Product of all sizes of the subsequent axes.

field.name: refEntityName
field.description: The entity name this axis maps to, e.g. moqui.math.ParameterLog, moqui.math.Parameter, etc.

entity.description: Stores tensor elements. Used only for row-wise access.

field.name: parentElementId
field.description: Link to the element if this is derived from it.

field.name: linearIndex
field.description: Flattened index computed with row-major stride formula.

field.name: indicesJson
field.description: Optional readable indices. JSON array with explicit coordinate, useful for debugging. Matches Google TensorStore schema.

entity.description: Stores the individual coordinate for each dimension of a tensor element, allowing N-dimensional indexing without calculating from linear index.

field.name: axisIndex
field.description: The index of the dimension (e.g., 0 for first, 1 for second). Corresponds to TensorAxis.axisIndex.

field.name: dimensionValue
field.description: The coordinate value along this specific axis for the tensor element.

entity.description: Defines a coordinate system for a given vector space. A coordinate system determines how vectors/points are represented (via a basis, and optional an origin).

field.name: parentSystemId
field.description: This field allows you to model coordinate system hierarchies. Set this field if this is a derived coordinate system. If you start with a standard coordinate system (e.g., a 3D Cartesian system) and create a rotated or shifted version, you can mark the new one as a child of the original. This helps track how new coordinate systems are related to existing ones. Versioning and Variants: if you have multiple coordinate systems that evolve from a common ancestor, the parent-child relationship makes it easier to understand and navigate the hierarchy.

field.name: originVectorId
field.description: Origin for affine systems.

entity.description: Defines the basis vectors that make up a coordinate system. The number of basis vectors must match the dimension of the vector space type. Optionally, each basis vector can be linked to a dimension label and a semantic purpose.

field.name: vectorPurposeEnumId
field.description: Semantic tag like 'basis', 'orthonormal', etc.

field.name: baseIndex
field.description: Order of the basis vector.

field.name: dimensionEnumId
field.description: Label the dimension this basis vector corresponds to (e.g., DimX, DimY, DimZ).

entity.description: Represents an algebraic transformation, functions composition, etc. e.g.: linear or affine transformation, typically defined by a matrix operation applied to vectors or other matrices. Transformations include coordinate changes, projections, scaling, rotations, and symbolic operations. This entity allows recording, naming, and semantically organizing such operations in the mathematical model.

field.name: parentTransformationId
field.description: When this transformation is itself the result of another composition, point back to the composite.

field.name: transformationTypeEnumId
field.description: Type of the transformation (e.g., affine, matrix inversion, eigen decomposition).

field.name: purposeEnumId
field.description: Semantic purpose or usage context of the transformation (e.g., coordinate frame conversion, geometric modeling, symbolic simplification).

field.name: name
field.description: Human-readable name or label for the transformation (e.g., "World to Robot Base").

field.name: lastApplicationDate
field.description: Timestamp of the most recent use or evaluation of this transformation (optional analytical metadata).

moqui.basic.Enumeration.description: Rotate 90° k times around the last two axes
Vector or Matrix Norm, Frobenius, Nuclear, etc.
Element-wise natural log: Yᵢ = ln xᵢ
Matrix Application to a Vector / Matrix
Geometrical Transformation (shearing, rotation, reflection, scaling, etc.)

entity.description: N-ary operand list for generic transformation. One row per operand (matrix, vector, tensor, etc.).

field.name: axisOffset
field.description: k = 0 main diag, +k super-diag, –k sub-diag.

entity.description: Defines a specific sub-tensor, a slice, of a larger N-dimensional tensor by its bounding box or specific indices.

field.name: sliceDefinitionJson
field.description: JSON string defining the slice parameters. Example: [{"axis": 0, "start": 0, "end": 10}, {"axis": 1, "index": 5}]. This allows flexible definition for different slicing methods (ranges, specific indices, steps).

entity.description: Triangular extraction (upper / lower).
Band extraction (general l / u).
Support block matrices extraction rules, which are matrices divided into smaller sub-matrices.

moqui.basic.Enumeration.description: Block Matrix Extraction (submatrix) from a Matrix
Grid or Regular Blocking Extraction. The matrix is partitioned into blocks by both rows and columns.
Horizontal Blocking Extraction. The matrix is split into horizontal strips (blocks). Each block contains a set of rows.
Vertical Blocking Extraction. The matrix is split into vertical strips (blocks). Each block contains a set of columns.
Hierarchical or Recursive Blocking. The matrix is split into vertical strips (blocks). A matrix is recursively divided into smaller blocks within blocks, creating a hierarchical structure.
Diagonal Blocking Extraction. The matrix is divided into blocks along the diagonal, with each block being a square or rectangular submatrix. Off-diagonal blocks are typically zero.
Symmetric Blocking Extraction. Blocks are extracted in such a way that if the original matrix is symmetric, the block structure also respects this symmetry.
Irregular Blocking Extraction. The matrix is divided into blocks that may have different sizes, depending on specific criteria, such as data distribution or computational efficiency.
Domain-Specific Blocking Extraction. The blocking pattern is chosen based on the application domain, such as signal processing, image processing, or finite element methods.

entity.description: Store Singular Value Decomposition (SVD), U/Σ/V, L/U, Q/R, etc. produced by a matrix factorisation. They are critical operations in linear algebra, often used in dimensionality reduction, to decompose and invert a matrix.

moqui.basic.Enumeration.description: Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) on a Matrix
Truncated Singular Value Decomposition (SVD). It is used primarily in data compression, information retrieval.
Economy Singular Value Decomposition (SVD). It removes redundant zero rows or columns from U and Sigma matrices.

entity.description: High-order tensor factorisation like CP, Tucker, HOSVD, TT, etc.
One factor matrix/tensor per mode for the parent TensorDecomposition. Supports CP (rank-1 factors) and Tucker (mode matrices).

field.name: reductionDimensions
field.description: JSON list of axes collapsed, e.g. [0] or [0,1]. Specifies which dimension or dimensions of input to calculate the norm across. If dimensions is None, the norm will be calculated across all dimensions of input. It must match the norm order.

field.name: keepDimensions
field.description: whether the output tensors have dimensions retained or not.

entity.description: Defines a transformation from one coordinate system to another. It is a coordinate-frame change expressed as matrix + two frames. Used for converting vector representations between frames.
An approximated numerical function. It model the numerical or computable aspect of a function. The associative/ideal aspect of a pure analytical function is modeled with the Transformation entity.

field.name: interpolationEnumId
field.description: The interpolation method for the approximated function, parametric path, etc. (e.g., polynomial, cycloidal).

field.name: vectorSpaceEnumId
field.description: Specifies the abstract vector space in which this function or path is defined (e.g., 2D, 3D, Quaternion space). It defines the dimensionality and mathematical rules but does not dictate coordinate representation.

field.name: codomainTypeEnumId
field.description: Type of the function value (scalar, vector, matrix, tensor, mixed).

field.name: parametrizationDegree
field.description: The number of independent parameters the path is defined over (e.g., 1 for time, 2 for surface u,v).

field.name: modeledTransformationId
field.description: Transformation approximated by this function (optional). Leave empty when the map is unknown or empirical.

field.name: maxRelativeError
field.description: Maximum relative error between the numeric function and the reference Transformation (e.g.: ‖f_num−f_exact‖/‖f_exact‖ or others norm).

field.name: errorMatrixId
field.description: Reference to a matrix/tensor representing the error distribution (by point, by direction, covariance matrix, etc.).

moqui.basic.Enumeration.description: Path Based on the Fourier Series Expansion
External Blob, only for parametric paths and trajectories (CSV, GIS Shapefile, …)

entity.description: An approximated function sample defined in an abstract vector space.

field.name: sampleTypeEnumId
field.description: Type of sample (e.g., waypoint, control point, corner point, intersection, break, safety stop, etc.).

field.name: sampleCode
field.description: The unique code of the sample.

field.name: sampleName
field.description: The symbolic name of the sample.

field.name: sequenceNum
field.description: Sequence number that expresses the order of the sample along the approximated function.

field.name: sampleVectorId
field.description: Vector representing this sample in the vector space. It could be a mixed quantities vector. Each vector component is a quantity like X, Y, Z positions and time or temperature, pressure, volume, energy, etc. or other quantities representing the state of a system in the state space.

field.name: sampleMatrixId
field.description: Matrix value at this sample (for matrix-valued functions).

entity.description: First/higher-order derivatives as linear maps (Jacobian/Hessian/etc.).

field.name: purposeEnumId
field.description: Specifies the semantic meaning of this derivative (e.g., tangent matrix, acceleration Hessian, curvature Tensor). All derivatives here are treated as linear maps, not as vector values.

field.name: derivativeOrder
field.description: The mathematical order of the derivative (1 = first derivative, 2 = second derivative, etc.).

field.name: derivativeMatrixId
field.description: Linear map (matrix) for the derivative (optional if tensor provided).

moqui.basic.Enumeration.description: First derivative matrix (e.g., Jacobian or directional velocity matrix). Includes geometric derivatives or velocity transformation (∂position/∂parameter, ∂position/∂t).
Second derivative matrix (e.g., Hessian or acceleration transformation). Describes how direction or velocity changes. Includes acceleration (∂²position/∂parameter², ∂²position/∂t²).
Third derivative matrix (e.g., rate of change of acceleration or binormal transformation).
Fourth derivative matrix (snap-like linear transformation). Often used in smooth motion generation or symbolic modeling.
Curvature tensor matrix. Represents curvature behavior for multiparameter paths or geometric domains.
Matrix representation of torsion or twisting behavior along the path (symbolic or 3D geometric interpretation).
Plane matrix spanned by tangent and normal vectors at the point (symbolic representation of the osculating plane).
Matrix form representing geodesic curvature on curved surfaces (i.e., deviation from intrinsic path).
Surface normal expressed as a matrix transformation (e.g., from tangent space to world space).
High-order tensor for jerk (3rd derivative) with symbolic or multi-parameter domains.
Symbolic or multi-axis matrix for 4th derivative behavior (snap-like change in acceleration direction).

entity.description: This entity represents the abstract and reusable definition of the model logic.

field.name: parentModelDefId
field.description: To represent a hierarchical relationship between models.

field.name: releaseStatusId
field.description: The release status.

moqui.basic.StatusFlow.description: Default lifecycle of a math model definition (PLM).

entity.description: External-system identifier for a MathModelDef (Hugging Face, OpenAI, MLflow, etc.).

field.name: externalSystemEnumId
field.description: The external registry / provider this identification refers to. See MathModelExternalSystem enumeration.

field.name: externalId
field.description: Identifier of the model in the external system. Examples: HF_HUB : "meta-llama/Llama-3-8B" OPENAI_API : "gpt-4-turbo-2024-04-09" OPENAI_API : "text-embedding-3-large" ANTHROPIC_API : "claude-opus-4-7" MLFLOW : "models:/MyRegisteredModel/3" WANDB : "myproject/run-id" ONNX_ZOO : "vision/classification/resnet/model/resnet50-v2-7"

field.name: externalVersion
field.description: Optional version / revision / commit hash on the external system (e.g. HF revision, ONNX opset version, MLflow stage version).

field.name: externalUri
field.description: Canonical URI to fetch / inspect the external record. Examples: https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Llama-3-8B https://api.openai.com/v1/models/text-embedding-3-large https://api.anthropic.com/v1/models/claude-opus-4-7

field.name: isPrimary
field.description: Y/N. Marks the preferred / canonical external reference for this model when more than one applies.

moqui.basic.Enumeration.description: Google AI / Vertex AI / Gemini API

entity.description: Link one or more scripts to the math model. It is also possible to associate multiple files to the model: Scripts: Python, R, MATLAB, etc. for procedural implementations. Configuration files: YAML, JSON for fixed parameters, neural network definitions (e.g. TensorFlow GraphDefs). Symbolic formulas: SymPy, Mathematica expression files.
Defines an instance of a computational model, including supporting graph structure and mesh for domain discretization.

field.name: basisOrder
field.description: For higher-order FEM/SEM or spectral bases (p-refinement).

entity.description: Execution instance of a MathModel (analysis, training, prediction, etc.).

field.name: approximatedFunctionId
field.description: A trajectory produced or consumed by the run of the model. A row in the Trajectory entity that stores the ordered sample list - times -> state-vectors, curve in phase-space, optimisation search path, etc. For ODE/PDE integrations, transient FEM/FVM, Kalman filters, adaptive dashboards, etc. After each solver step get (t, state) pairs, store them under a single trajectory entry to be able to: replay the run, post-process (FFT, energy, stability), compare two runs (error norms, convergence). For optimisation / MCMC / control, the trajectory can be gradient-descent steps in weight-space, chain of accepted samples in MCMC, closed-loop set-point evolution for a controller. For machine-learning training, if you log training loss vs. epoch or learning-rate schedules as a path, later it is possible to plot or analyse it without cracking open binary checkpoints. For static (steady-state) runs the field stays empty.

entity.description: Fine-grained log/events emitted by the solver while the run is in progress.
Simple performance counters recorded at the end of a run.
A snowflake table for connecting mathematical objects to the model.

field.name: generatedByRunId
field.description: Which run generated or last overwrote this record.

field.name: uomId
field.description: Physical units.

moqui.basic.Enumeration.description: Category of small sets: objects = sets, morphisms = functions
Category of Groups and Group Homomorphisms
Real Vector Spaces (finite dimension) with Linear Maps
Complex Vector Spaces (finite dimension) with Linear Maps
Big Category of Small Categories + Functors

entity.description: An object of a category; polymorphic reference into any entity.
A morphism f : A → B.

moqui.basic.Enumeration.description: N-ary Composition (f1 o f2 o ... o fn)

entity.description: Abstract graph data structure. Can model directed/undirected, weighted/unweighted graphs. Serves as parent for meshes, dependency graphs, scene‑graphs, etc. It is a the base of mesh related entities and it can be usefull to model a device network.

field.name: parentGraphId
field.description: Used to model subgraph of a graph.

field.name: graphTypeEnumId
field.description: Directed, undirected, multigraph, hypergraph, etc.

field.name: vectorSpaceEnumId
field.description: The graph is embedded in the vector space is each vertex is assigned a vector in that space.

field.name: graphOrder
field.description: The order of a graph is its number of vertices.

field.name: size
field.description: The size of a graph is its number of edges.

field.name: maxDegree
field.description: The max degree or valency for any vertex. It is the maximum number of edges that are incident to any vertex, where a loop is counted twice. The degree of a graph is the maximum of the degrees of its vertices.

field.name: incidenceFunctionServiceName
field.description: An incidence function is a service for mapping every edge to an unordered pair of vertices. The mapping result is stored in the GraphEdge entity. Only used for undirected multigraph.

field.name: loopsAllowed
field.description: Indicates if the graph can contain loops.

field.name: isTrivial
field.description: A graph with only one vertex and no edges.

field.name: isRegular
field.description: A graph whose vertices all have the same degree.

field.name: isEdgeless
field.description: A graph with only vertices and no edges.

field.name: isEmpty
field.description: The graph with no vertices and no edges.

field.name: isVertexTransitive
field.description: A graph that has symmetries that map any vertex to any other vertex.

field.name: isConnected
field.description: Indicates whether the graph is connected which happens if there is a path of edges connecting every two vertices.

field.name: isPlanar
field.description: Whether the graph can be embedded in the plane without edge crossings or intersections.

field.name: isWeighted
field.description: A weighted graph or a network is a graph in which a weight is assigned to each edge.

field.name: adjacencyMatrixId
field.description: A square matrix used to represent adjacency. Given n vertices build n*n matrix of adjacency information: Entry (i,j) is true value if vertices i and j are adjacent. The elements of the matrix indicate whether pairs of vertices are adjacent or not in the graph.

entity.description: Optional binary-encoded graph (GraphML, GraphSON, Protobuf, Arrow IPC). Must be used when GraphVertex / GraphEdge rows don't exist.
Vertex or node in a graph. May be symbolic or spatial. Represents a point, state, or entity in the graph structure.

field.name: degree
field.description: The degree of a vertex is the number of edges incident to it. For an undirected graph, the degree of a vertex is equal to the number of adjacent vertices. A special case is a loop, which adds two to the degree. For a directed graph, a loop adds one to the in degree and one to the out degree. An isolated vertex is a vertex with degree zero; that is, a vertex that is not an endpoint of any edge. A leaf vertex is a vertex with degree one.

field.name: isCutVertex
field.description: A vertex the removal of which would disconnect the remaining graph.

field.name: notBelongToEdge
field.description: A vertex may exist in a graph and not belong to an edge.

field.name: positionVectorId
field.description: Optional geometric or abstract position of the node, if applicable (e.g., for graph layout or visualization).

entity.description: Edge connecting two vertices in a graph. May include weight and direction.

field.name: edgeTypeEnumId
field.description: Semantic or geometric type of the edge (e.g., sharp, smooth, boundary).

field.name: isLoop
field.description: A loop is an edge that joins a vertex to itself.

field.name: length
field.description: Precomputed geometric length or measure of the edge.

field.name: weight
field.description: Optional weight or cost associated with this edge.

entity.description: A mesh is a specialized graph with additional element definitions. Usually it is a polygon mesh built upon a graph structure. It represents a discrete geometric object composed of vertices, edges, and cells. It contains geometric and rendering information and is used in digital geometry processing, graphics, simulation (CAD) or finite element analysis (FEM).

field.name: parentMeshId
field.description: Reference to the mesh this adapted mesh was derived from (h-, r-, p-type refinement, etc.).

field.name: adaptationTypeEnumId
field.description: Type of mesh adaptation performed (e.g., refinement, coarsening, smoothing).

field.name: sourcePathId
field.description: Optional reference to a parametric path from which the mesh was generated.

field.name: orientationEnumId
field.description: Indicates whether the order of vertices and edges of an element are listed clockwise/right‑handed vs anticlockwise/left‑handed. For a surface mesh this is the order of polygon vertices. In a volume mesh (tetrahedra, hexahedra, prisms, etc.) it depends on the Jacobian sign: - A right‑handed ordering gives a positive Jacobian determinant and outward element normals. - A left‑handed ordering flips the elements.

field.name: totalCellMeasure
field.description: Total measure of all k-cells depending on their dimension (length, area, volume, etc.)

moqui.basic.Enumeration.description: h-type Refinement (refine by adding smaller cells)
h-type Derefinement (remove cells in low-error regions)
r-type Refinement (move/displace nodes without adding cells)
Adaptive Remeshing (recreate mesh in selected region)
rh-type Refinement (combine h- and r- refinement)
Automatic Adaptation Based on Error Estimation

entity.description: Optional binary-encoded mesh (e.g. glTF-bin, STL, VTK, PBF). Must be used when MeshKCell rows don't exist.
Mesh quality statistics.
Generalized k-dimensional cell of a mesh (e.g. vertex, edge, face, volume, hypervolume).

field.name: dimension
field.description: K-cell dimension: 0 = vertex, 1 = edge, 2 = face, etc.

field.name: measure
field.description: K-Cell area, volume, etc.

field.name: isBoundary
field.description: Indicates whether the cell lies on a mesh boundary.

field.name: orientationEnumId
field.description: Can override the default orientation of the mesh for this cell.

field.name: normalVectorId
field.description: A vector perpendicular to the cell, often precomputed or derived from vertex positions. The normal vector represents the orientation of the cell. Used for lighting (shading), collision detection, and backface culling. Only for dimension greater than 1.

field.name: centroidVectorId
field.description: The geometric center of the cell. Only for dimension > 1.

entity.description: Associates a specific texture and its mapping with a MeshKCell. This allows precise UV mapping and transformation per cell, useful in graphical or texture-based simulations.

field.name: uvCoordVectorId
field.description: UV/Texture coordinates used to map 2D textures onto the surface of the cell. Defines how a texture is wrapped around this specific k-cell.

field.name: uvTransformationId
field.description: Transformation applied to the UV coordinates before texture lookup. Commonly used UVs manipulation for texture tiling, offseting, rotation or procedural variations.

field.name: textureUrl
field.description: URL or path to the external texture image (e.g., PNG, JPEG). Used by rendering engines or post-processors to fetch and apply the image.

field.name: colorVectorId
field.description: RGB or RGBA color for the entire cell used in flat shading.

entity.description: Mesh k-cell quality statistics.

Connects MeshKCell to one or more underlying graph vertices. - If the mesh k-cell dimension = 0, only one GraphVertex is allowed (single association). - If the mesh k-cell dimension is greater than 0, allows multiple graph vertices to define the vertices of the cell (ordered multiple association).

field.name: sequenceNum
field.description: Sequence index to sort vertices in cells of size greater than 0.

field.name: isUniqueReference
field.description: Indicates whether this is the only vertex associated with the cell (required if dimension=0). - 'Y' for vertex cells (0-cells). - 'N' for cells with multiple vertices (cells greater than 0).

entity.description: Associates a mesh 1-cell to a graph edge.

Defines the oriented incidence between two k-cells, such as a face contained within a volume. The higher cell and lower cell refer to k-cells with dimensions that strictly satisfy the relation: higherCell.dimension = lowerCell.dimension + 1
A named group of mesh k-cells for defining selections, physics domains, or export purposes.

field.name: mathModelId
field.description: math model from which the groups of cells is derived.

moqui.basic.Enumeration.description: Region of the mesh where thermal equations are applied (e.g. temperature simulation).
Region where structural (mechanical stress/strain) equations are applied.
Region used for fluid dynamics simulation (CFD).
Region used for electromagnetic simulation (e.g. Maxwell equations).
Region supporting coupled physics models (e.g. thermal-structural).
Zone where initial values are applied (e.g. initial temperature, velocity, etc).
Control domain for active feedback, model-predictive control, or sensor influence.
Region used to sample or extract data (e.g. for statistics or averaging).
Area where loads / sources are applied
Cells selected by proximity to a ParametricPath

entity.description: Associates individual mesh k-cells with a mesh group.

field.name: materialEnumId
field.description: >Material used to compute or interpret this parameter (e.g., steel, glass).

field.name: shaderProfileEnumId
field.description: Shading model (e.g., Phong, Lambert) to interpret visual/rendering parameters.

entity.description: An abstract parametric path representing the parameterized evolution in any abstract space, relative to a coordinate system or reference frame. It can represent a spatial, thermodynamic, economic, or logical state-space path. ParametricPath is vector-based so set ApproximatedFunction.codomainTypeEnumId = AfcVector and sampleVectorId for samples.

field.name: parentPathId
field.description: If you want to allow hierarchical/compound paths. Used for example to represent a kinematic chain that connects all trajectories using the parent relations. All motion is connected to the motion of the parent. The first node in the chain will not have a parent.

field.name: profileEnumId
field.description: Defines the calculation profile applied to traverse the parametric path. Usually it is the timing law or motion profile specifying how movement progresses along the path.

field.name: isClosed
field.description: Indicates whether the path forms a closed loop.

field.name: coordinateSystemId
field.description: The coordinate system or reference frame used for the path definition, if any (e.g., Cartesian, Polar, Spherical). If absent, we still know the path is in a known dimension. This overrides the parent path's coordinate system, if one exists.

field.name: coordTransformationId
field.description: Defines the transformation applied to the parametric path to map it from a parent coordinate system to its local frame. This includes translation, rotation, or both. At a minimum, either a translation or rotation must be specified. Only one of coordinate or transformation can be specified. The service layer logic must enforce this rule.

field.name: compositionMethodEnumId
field.description: Defines the method used to blend this path with its sibling paths in a compound path, specified with the parentPathId field. The composition follows the order specified by the compositionSequenceNum field. The parentPathId must not be empty.

field.name: compositionSequenceNum
field.description: Defines the sequence number of this path within a compound path. Lower numbers are executed first.

moqui.basic.Enumeration.description: PLCopen Aborting - abort current segment, start next immediately
PLCopen Buffered - queue next segment, start after current completes
PLCopen Blending Low - blend at the lower of the two segment velocities
PLCopen Blending Previous - blend at velocity of previous segment
PLCopen Blending Next - blend at velocity of next segment
PLCopen Blending High - blend at the higher of the two segment velocities
PLCopen TMNone - exact stop at the point, no geometric blending
PLCopen TMCornerDistance - blend within corner radius (ParametricPathPoint.tolerance)
PLCopen TMConstantVelocity - maintain constant velocity through transition
PLCopen TMStartVelocity - continue at current speed into next segment
PLCopen TMMaxVelocity - maximize velocity through the transition

entity.description: Parametric path data, which may include waypoints or geometric path points, timing, velocity, acceleration profiles, and control parameters to import. Must be used when ParametricPathPoint rows don't exist.

moqui.basic.Enumeration.description: G-code (Numerical Control Programming Language) Parametric Path Data

entity.description: Points of a parametric path in an abstract space.

field.name: isCriticalPoint
field.description: Indicates whether this point is critical for path integrity (e.g., safety stop point, emergency stop point, start/end points).

field.name: tolerance
field.description: Allowed deviation from this point during execution, expressed in the same unit as the path space.

field.name: arcLength
field.description: Cumulative distance from the start-point; useful for feed-rate, progress bar, time-length mapping.

field.name: weight
field.description: Rational weight for NURBS/Rational Bézier.

entity.description: Event bound to a parametric path.

field.name: eventTypeEnumId
field.description: Type of event (e.g., Start, Stop, Custom Action).

field.name: eventPayload
field.description: Additional data or instructions related to the event.

field.name: triggerPoint
field.description: The approximated point along the path where this event is triggered.

field.name: isTriggerable
field.description: Can this event be triggered manually?

entity.description: Time-parameterized specialization of ParametricPath or motion law, relative to a coordinate system or reference frame. For mechanical components the motion specifies the kinematic chain. It is a specialized form of ParametricPath where time is the explicit, primary parameter of evolution. This entity models time-dependent motion or processes, such as the trajectory of a mechanical component, a robot joint, or a thermodynamic transition. It includes fields for velocity, acceleration, jerk, and snap vectors, all of which are derived with respect to time. Use `Trajectory` when your parametric path needs to represent real-time dynamics, motion profiles, or control laws tied to temporal evolution. It is a time-parameterized specialization of ParametricPath and inherits its points.

field.name: actuationTypeEnumId
field.description: Indicates whether the trajectory is actuated directly or indirectly as a result of other motion (e.g., virtual or derived motion).

field.name: actuatorTypeEnumId
field.description: The constraint on the actuators type required for executing this trajectory. The actuator that produces force, torque, or displacement, usually in a controlled way, when an electrical, pneumatic or hydraulic input is supplied to it in a system (called an actuating system). An actuator converts such an input signal into the required form of mechanical energy or motion and can be of types like revolute, prismatic, or fixed. It is a type of transducer. In simple terms, it is a "mover".

field.name: controlMethodEnumId
field.description: Specifies the control algorithm or method used by a device to execute this trajectory (e.g., PID, open-loop, model predictive control).

moqui.basic.Enumeration.description: Direct - Movement is initiated by the component
Indirect - Motion derived from a parent component or external force
None - No actuation of this axis
Virtual - Motion is computed and used for expressing an imaginary or derived movement
Continuous - Revolves around an axis with a continuous range of motion (unlimited motion)
Prismatic - Linear sliding motion along an axis within a fixed range
Revolute - Rotates around an axis within a fixed range of motion

entity.description: The points, or discrete samples, on a trajectory followed by e.g. an axis along or around which a device moves relative to a coordinate system.

field.name: isBreakPoint
field.description: Indicates if this point represents a break period.

field.name: breakDuration
field.description: Duration of the break at this point (in seconds or milliseconds).

field.name: breakReason
field.description: Optional description for why the break is required (e.g., cooling, safety check).

field.name: blendingEnumId
field.description: Per-point blending override (ParametricPathCompositionMethod values). If set, overrides ParametricPath.compositionMethodEnumId for the transition into this point. isBreakPoint=Y takes priority (always an exact stop regardless of this field). For PpcmPlcTMCornerDist the blend radius comes from ParametricPathPoint.tolerance.

field.name: pointTimeOffsetMillis
field.description: Planned time offset (millis) for reaching this point. The time, relative to the origin of time axis, to which the trajectory should interpolate or approximate this point during execution. Each point in a trajectory will correspond to a particular timestamp and the corresponding point vector.

field.name: actualPointVectorId
field.description: Vector representing the actual position reached during execution, for deviation analysis.

field.name: breakDeviation
field.description: Difference between planned and actual break time.

field.name: positionError
field.description: The deviation between the actual and intended position at this point.

field.name: velocityError
field.description: The deviation in velocity compared to the planned trajectory.

field.name: accelerationError
field.description: The difference in acceleration compared to the planned value.

field.name: jerkError
field.description: The deviation in jerk (third derivative) at this point.

field.name: snapError
field.description: The deviation in snap (fourth derivative) at this point.

field.name: compositeError
field.description: A weighted aggregate of position, velocity, and acceleration errors for overall analysis: compositeError = (positionWeight * positionError + velocityWeights * velocityError + accelerationWeight * accelerationError) / (positionWeight + velocityWeight + accelerationWeight)

field.name: hasPointExecutionFailure
field.description: Point execution failure.

field.name: hasPointExecutionDeviation
field.description: Point execution deviation.

field.name: vibrationLevel
field.description: If you measure instantaneous vibrations at each point.

field.name: maxPowerDraw
field.description: Maximum power draw at or near this point (Watts). Identifies potential overloading risks.

field.name: maxTemperature
field.description: Temperature at or near this point execution (°C). Monitors thermal stress on the system.

field.name: jitter
field.description: Only for period or cyclical trajectories.

field.name: avgCompositeError
field.description: Weighted average of position, velocity, and acceleration errors for high-level analysis.

field.name: pointSuccessRate
field.description: The percentage of successfully executed trajectory points (no error or deviation).

field.name: reliability
field.description: The probability of completing the trajectory without failure (per-run basis).

field.name: settlingTime
field.description: Time taken for the system to remain within a predefined error margin around the target value.

field.name: riseTime
field.description: Time taken for the trajectory to rise from 10% to 90% of its final target value.

field.name: peakTime
field.description: Time to reach the maximum value during the trajectory execution.

field.name: steadyStateError
field.description: Persistent error between the desired and actual values once the system has settled.

field.name: overshootPercentage
field.description: The percentage by which the system exceeded the intended value during execution. Overshoot (%) = [(Max Value - Target Value) / Target Value] x 100

field.name: integralAbsoluteError
field.description: The Integral of Absolute Error (IAE) for the trajectory, representing the sum of absolute deviations over time.

field.name: integralSquaredError
field.description: The Integral of Squared Error (ISE), giving more weight to larger deviations in the trajectory.

field.name: integralTimeAbsoluteError
field.description: The Integral of Time-weighted Absolute Error (ITAE), penalizing long-lasting errors.

field.name: integralTimeSquaredError
field.description: The Integral of Time-weighted Squared Error (ITSE), amplifying persistent large deviations.

field.name: accuracy
field.description: Closeness of the actual trajectory to the planned one.

field.name: pathDeviationIndex
field.description: Ratio of actual path length to planned path length. A value greater then 1 indicates inefficiency. Path Deviation Index = Actual Path Length / Planned Path Length

field.name: circularityError
field.description: Maximum radial deviation from an ideal circle.

field.name: linearityError
field.description: Maximum deviation from an ideal straight line.

field.name: naturalFrequency
field.description: The frequency at which the system naturally oscillates (in Hz).

field.name: dampingRatio
field.description: The damping ratio, indicating how quickly oscillations decay.

field.name: powerSpectralDensity
field.description: The power spectral density of the trajectory motion, used to analyze vibrations and oscillations and for identifying vibration patterns.

field.name: harmonicDistortion
field.description: Percentage of distortion caused by higher-order harmonics.

field.name: vibrationLevel
field.description: If you store an overall RMS or peak vibration for the entire run.

field.name: inertia
field.description: The property of an object to resist change in velocity unless acted upon by any outside force. Higher inertia objects require larger torques to accelerate and decelerate. Inertia is dependent upon the mass and shape of an object. Inertia should always be considered when sizing motors for motion systems.

field.name: friction
field.description: A resistance to motion caused by contact with a surface. Friction can be constant with varying speed (Coulomb friction) or proportional to speed (Viscous friction).

field.name: resonance
field.description: Oscillation of a system following a sudden change in state.

field.name: cogging
field.description: A property used to describe non-uniform angular velocity. Cogging appears as a jumpy motion, especially at low speeds.

field.name: energyConsumption
field.description: Total energy consumed during trajectory execution (in Joules or kWh) for efficiency analysis.

field.name: controlEffort
field.description: Total control input (e.g., torque, force) used to execute the trajectory.

field.name: efficiency
field.description: The ratio of power output to power input.

field.name: maxPowerDraw
field.description: Maximum power draw during trajectory execution (in Watts). Identifies potential overloading risks.

field.name: maxTemperature
field.description: Highest recorded temperature during execution (in °C). Monitors thermal stress on the system.

field.name: statsName
field.description: The name of this trajectory statistics.

field.name: totalSquaredDurationMillis
field.description: Sum of squared durations for incremental std. dev calc.

field.name: totalSquaredLatency
field.description: Sum of squared latencies for incremental std. dev.

field.name: totalSquaredJitter
field.description: Sum of squared jitters for incremental std. dev.

field.name: totalSquaredDisplacement
field.description: Sum of squared displacements for incremental std. dev.

field.name: totalSquaredVelocity
field.description: Sum of squared velocities for incremental std. dev.

field.name: totalSquaredAcceleration
field.description: Sum of squared accelerations for incremental std. dev.

field.name: totalSquaredJerk
field.description: Sum of squared jerks for incremental std. dev.

field.name: totalSquaredSnap
field.description: Sum of squared snaps for incremental std. dev.

field.name: totalRuns
field.description: Total number of completed trajectory executions considered for statistics.

field.name: totalSuccessRuns
field.description: Total number of successful runs without critical errors or deviations.

field.name: averageSuccessRate
field.description: Average success rate across all executions (percentage of successful trajectory points).

field.name: meanTimeBetweenFailures
field.description: Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) in hours, indicating the average operating time between system failures. MTBF = Total Uptime / Number of Failures

field.name: meanTimeToRepair
field.description: Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) in hours, indicating the average downtime per failure. MTTR = Total Downtime / Number of Repairs

field.name: availability
field.description: System availability calculated as MTBF / (MTBF + MTTR).

field.name: probabilityFailureDemand
field.description: Probability that the system will fail upon demand.

field.name: failureRate
field.description: Failure rate per unit time (e.g., failures/hour).

field.name: riskPriorityNumber
field.description: Product of severity, occurrence, and detection rankings for risk prioritization.

field.name: accuracy
field.description: Closeness of the actual trajectory to the planned one.

field.name: precision
field.description: Closeness of repeated trajectory executions under unchanged conditions.

field.name: repeatability
field.description: A statistical measure of how consistently the system returns to the same position over repeated trajectories.

field.name: repeatabilityStdDev
field.description: Maximum deviation observed across multiple repeated trajectory runs or statistical spread of the same measurements. In practice, measured as the difference between the farthest points or the largest standard deviation from the nominal repeated trajectory under identical conditions.

field.name: avgEnergyConsumption
field.description: Total energy consumed during trajectory execution (in Joules or kWh) for efficiency analysis.

field.name: avgControlEffort
field.description: Total control input (e.g., torque, force) used to execute the trajectory.

field.name: totalSquaredEfficiency
field.description: Sum of squared efficiencies for incremental std. dev.

field.name: maxPowerDraw
field.description: Maximum power draw during trajectory execution (in Watts). Identifies potential overloading risks.

field.name: maxTemperature
field.description: Highest recorded temperature during execution (in °C). Monitors thermal stress on the system.

field.name: naturalFrequency
field.description: The frequency at which the system naturally oscillates (in Hz).

field.name: avgDampingRatio
field.description: The damping ratio, indicating how quickly oscillations decay.

field.name: avgPowerSpectralDensity
field.description: The power spectral density of the trajectory motion, used to analyze vibrations and oscillations and for identifying vibration patterns.

field.name: avgHarmonicDistortion
field.description: Percentage of distortion caused by higher-order harmonics.

field.name: totalSquaredInertia
field.description: Sum of squared inertia for incremental std. dev.

field.name: totalSquaredFriction
field.description: Sum of squared frictions for incremental std. dev.

field.name: totalSquaredResonance
field.description: Sum of squared resonances for incremental std. dev.

field.name: totalSquaredCogging
field.description: Sum of squared coggings for incremental std. dev.
